name: Cal.com API
what_it_does: Open-source scheduling infrastructure API for booking meetings, checking availability, and managing calendars — a developer-first alternative to Calendly with MCP server support.
use_cases: Booking meetings on behalf of users via agent workflows
Checking real-time availability across connected calendars before proposing times
Managing event types, durations, and scheduling constraints programmatically
Embedding customizable booking flows into products via API + webhooks
Self-hosting scheduling infrastructure to keep calendar data in-house
not_for: Enterprise meeting-room and resource scheduling at scale (use Microsoft Bookings or Robin)
Businesses needing phone/SMS appointment reminders without custom setup
best_when: Agents or developers need programmatic scheduling with full control — especially when self-hosting or MCP integration is a requirement.
avoid_when: You need a no-code scheduling tool for non-technical teams; Cal.com's self-hosted setup and API-first approach adds operational overhead.

auth.notes: Cal.com Cloud uses API keys (per-user) and OAuth2 for third-party app access. Self-hosted instances use API keys. OAuth2 supports granular scopes for reading availability, booking, and managing event types. API v2 uses Bearer tokens.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free for individuals on Cal.com Cloud (unlimited bookings, core features); self-hosted is free under AGPL-3.0
pricing.paid_tiers: Teams from $12/user/month; Enterprise custom pricing; self-hosted Enterprise license available
pricing.notes: Self-hosted is free and fully featured under AGPL. Cal.com Cloud free tier is generous for individuals. Teams and org features require paid plans. API access included at all tiers.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; generous for typical scheduling workloads

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API errors are generally well-structured with HTTP status codes and descriptive messages. MCP server surfaces clear tool descriptions. Some edge cases in availability queries return undocumented 4xx responses.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Bookings are not idempotent by default — duplicate booking attempts create duplicate entries. Agents must check existing bookings before creating new ones. Cancellation and rescheduling are safe to retry.
known_agent_gotchas: MCP server requires a running Cal.com instance (cloud or self-hosted) — not a standalone service
Time zone handling is critical — all times should be passed in UTC or explicit tz offset; slot availability is tz-sensitive
OAuth tokens for third-party apps have short expiry — implement refresh token rotation
Event type IDs must be fetched dynamically — hardcoding them breaks across environments
Webhook signature verification required to confirm booking events are authentic
Self-hosted API v1 and Cal.com Cloud API v2 have diverging endpoint structures
